title: fix(siglip2): improve pooled output logic in modular file
body:
Fixes # (issue)

The PR description is largely a template and does not specify the exact bug/behavior change desired, beyond “improve pooled output logic in modular file”. Given the codebase, “pooled output” most directly refers to `pooler_output` produced by the vision/text encoders and consumed by `Siglip2Model` (contrastive) and by `Siglip2ForImageClassification` (classification, but it currently does its own pooling).

step_plan.summary: Improve SigLIP2 pooled output handling so vision pooler_output properly respects padding masks and is used consistently across contrastive and image-classification paths. Add robustness so feature extraction and forward passes behave predictably even when masks/head configuration would otherwise produce invalid pooled outputs.

steps.operation: Fix attention-mask expansion and semantics in probe-based pooling so padded tokens cannot influence the pooled embedding
steps.reason: The pooling head currently converts a 2D mask to 4D and then repeats/reshapes it; this is a common source of shape/broadcast mistakes (especially with target_len=1 probe) and can accidentally unmask padded tokens or produce an incorrect attn_mask shape for the underlying attention module.
steps.side_effects: May change numerical outputs for models using pixel_attention_mask (expected improvement). Needs verification for both FlashAttention2 and non-FA paths, and for attention implementations that expect specific attn_mask shapes/dtypes.

steps.operation: Ensure vision encoder always produces a correct, mask-aware pooler_output (and uses consistent mask form)
steps.reason: This is where pooler_output is created. To “improve pooled output logic”, the forward pass should pass the correct mask representation into the pooling head (and handle edge cases like missing/empty masks) so pooled embeddings remain invariant to padding and have stable shape/dtype.
steps.side_effects: Downstream consumers (Siglip2Model and image classification) will receive updated pooler_output values. If any callers relied on previous (buggy) padding influence, behavior will change. Should re-run vision forward with/without pixel_attention_mask and with FA2 enabled/disabled.

steps.operation: Make image-classification pooling consistent by reusing outputs.pooler_output when available (fallback to masked mean only when needed)
steps.reason: The classification head currently ignores the model’s pooler_output and does its own masked mean pooling, which can disagree with the contrastive pathway and can mishandle padding depending on mask shape. Reusing pooler_output aligns semantics across heads and centralizes pooling correctness in the pooling head.
steps.side_effects: Classification logits will change (expected, more consistent pooling). Must ensure classifier input dimension matches pooler_output hidden size. Add/adjust tests or at least sanity checks for both masked and unmasked inputs; ensure no division-by-zero if fallback mean pooling is used with fully-masked inputs.

steps.operation: Stabilize public image feature extraction by handling missing pooler_output (e.g., when head disabled) with a safe, mask-aware fallback
steps.reason: get_image_features currently returns vision_outputs.pooler_output directly. If pooled output logic changes or if use_head can be false in some configs, returning None would break the public API. Provide a fallback pooling strategy (consistent with classification/vision) to guarantee a (batch, hidden_size) tensor.
steps.side_effects: Changes behavior for configs where pooler_output would be None; could affect downstream pipelines that assumed head always exists. Requires careful handling of pixel_attention_mask to avoid padded-token leakage and to prevent NaNs for empty masks.

steps.operation: Harden contrastive forward against None/invalid pooled outputs and ensure embeddings are normalized safely
steps.reason: Siglip2Model.forward assumes vision_outputs.pooler_output and text_outputs.pooler_output are non-None and normalizes them. If pooled output computation is adjusted (or fallback paths are introduced), add validation and/or consistent fallback to prevent runtime errors and NaNs during normalization (especially when masks could produce degenerate vectors).
steps.side_effects: May introduce explicit error messages or fallback pooling for robustness. Any change affects contrastive logits and loss numerics; should validate that logits remain finite for padded inputs and that gradients still flow as expected.
